evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my empty ViewController: EmptyViewController.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyViewController.cs (new SparkScan(settings), SparkScanView.Create with this.View as parent, ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate, BarcodeScanned event or ISparkScanListener, ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear → PrepareScanning/StopScanning, IFrameData / ImageBuffers using-disposal), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription, AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching initialize for SDK 8.0+, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions calling ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching (required for SDK 8.0+)
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new SparkScanSettings() is used (not SparkScanSettings.Create(), which does not exist)
new SparkScan(settings) is used (not SparkScan.Create(...), which does not exist)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
SparkScanView.Create( is called with this.View (or this.View!) as the parentView argument — NOT a SparkScanCoordinatorLayout (which is Android-only)
SparkScanView.Create( receives the DataCaptureContext as the context argument
SparkScanView.Create( receives the SparkScan instance as the sparkScan argument
SparkScanView.Create( receives a SparkScanViewSettings as the settings argument
sparkScan.BarcodeScanned += handler is wired (or sparkScan.AddListener if using ISparkScanListener)
ViewWillAppear forwards to sparkScanView.PrepareScanning() (not sparkScanView.OnResume, which does not exist on iOS)
ViewWillDisappear forwards to sparkScanView.StopScanning() (not sparkScanView.OnPause)
OnPause / OnResume are NOT used (those are Android-only)
No call to BarcodeCapture.Create or barcodeCapture.AddListener appears (SparkScan is not the same API as BarcodeCapture)
No DataCaptureView is created (SparkScan has its own pre-built UI)
No BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created
No Camera.GetDefaultCamera() or dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(...) call is present (SparkScan manages its own camera)

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan to my existing .NET iOS ViewController. Here's the ViewController: EmptyViewController.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, log the barcode data.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates SparkScan into EmptyViewController.cs with QR and Data Matrix enabled. The BarcodeScanned handler logs the barcode data via Console.WriteLine or NSLog. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
new SparkScan( is used (not SparkScan.Create)
The BarcodeScanned event handler (or OnBarcodeScanned) reads args.Session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode or session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
barcode.Data is accessed
The scan result is logged (Console.WriteLine, System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine, NSLog, or similar)
ViewWillAppear forwards to sparkScanView.PrepareScanning()
ViewWillDisappear forwards to sparkScanView.StopScanning()
Setup checklist mentions Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription

evals.prompt: In my .NET iOS SparkScan app, I want to reject barcodes whose value starts with '000' and show an error message in the SparkScan UI for 5 seconds before scanning resumes. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate, builds a SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback and a SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback(message, resumeCapturingDelay), assigns the delegate to sparkScanView.Feedback, and returns the error feedback when barcode.Data starts with '000'.

assertions.text: The view controller (or a dedicated class) implements ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate
GetFeedbackForBarcode(Barcode barcode) is implemented
new SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( is constructed with a message argument and a resumeCapturingDelay argument
resumeCapturingDelay uses TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5) (or TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(5000)) — not a bare integer 5, not a double, not a TimeInterval
new SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() (or one of its multi-argument constructors) is used for the success case
sparkScanView.Feedback is assigned to the delegate instance (typically this)
GetFeedbackForBarcode inspects barcode.Data and returns the error feedback when it starts with '000'
GetFeedbackForBarcode does NOT call DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync or any UI dispatcher (the delegate runs on a background thread)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, PrepareScanning/StopScanning lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: Hide the torch and trigger buttons on my .NET iOS SparkScanView and add a custom UIButton that calls StartScanning() programmatically.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets sparkScanView.TorchControlVisible = false and sparkScanView.TriggerButtonVisible = false, then wires a UIButton's TouchUpInside handler to call sparkScanView.StartScanning(). Uses the renamed v7 property TorchControlVisible (not the v6 TorchButtonVisible).

assertions.text: sparkScanView.TorchControlVisible = false is set (not TorchButtonVisible, which was removed in v7)
sparkScanView.TriggerButtonVisible = false is set
sparkScanView.StartScanning() is called from a UIButton's TouchUpInside event handler (or equivalent control event)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored in my .NET iOS SparkScan app.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the SparkScanSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, not a double 0.5, not TimeInterval).

assertions.text: settings.CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not a double 0.5, not TimeInterval)
The value is applied to a SparkScanSettings instance (not BarcodeCaptureSettings)
Either CodeDuplicateFilter is set before new SparkScan(settings), or sparkScan.ApplySettingsAsync(settings) is invoked after the change
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm extracting a thumbnail of the scanned barcode by reading args.FrameData?.ImageBuffers in OnBarcodeScanned, but the SparkScan camera preview freezes after the first scan in my .NET iOS app. Help me fix it.
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies that the iOS .NET binding requires explicit disposal of every IFrameData and the ImageBuffer / FrameDataImage. Replaces ad-hoc access with `using var imageBuffer = args.FrameData?.ImageBuffers.LastOrDefault();` and `using var frame = imageBuffer?.ToImage();` patterns.

assertions.text: The skill identifies that IFrameData / ImageBuffer / FrameDataImage must be disposed on iOS
The skill explains that failing to dispose the frame causes a frozen / non-responsive / stuttering preview
The fix uses a `using var imageBuffer = args.FrameData?.ImageBuffers.LastOrDefault();` pattern (or an equivalent explicit Dispose() call)
If a UIImage / frame image is derived, a `using var frame = imageBuffer?.ToImage();` pattern is used
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, PrepareScanning/StopScanning lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET iOS SparkScan app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode via an async HTTP call, and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets sparkScan.Enabled = false at the start of the BarcodeScanned handler, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync. The IFrameData using-disposal is preserved.

assertions.text: sparkScan.Enabled = false is set inside the BarcodeScanned handler before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
sparkScan.Enabled = true is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup (not RunOnUiThread, which is Android-only)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, PrepareScanning/StopScanning lifecycle) is preserved
